In the presence of Union Home Minister and Minister of Cooperation Shri Amit Shah, the governments of Rajasthan and Haryana inks an important agreement for construction and implementation of Yamuna Water Project
articles.full_content:
With this agreement, the long-standing water-related problem of the people of Haryana and Rajasthan, which is almost 3 decades old, has been resolved today This agreement is an excellent example of the mantra ‘Solution through Dialogue’ given by Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi Prime Minister Modi Ji's message of ‘Cooperative Federalism’ has been truly realized by Haryana, Rajasthan, and the Ministry of Jal Shakti in its real spirit After this agreement, arrangements will be made to supply drinking water to the regions of Sikar, Churu, and Jhunjhunu in Rajasthan, along with Bhiwani and Fatehabad areas in Haryana The rainwater, which was previously going to waste and not being used by anyone, will now, after this agreement, quench people's thirst and will be stored in large ponds to help recharge the groundwater level This agreement is an excellent example of a Win-Win Situation for both Haryana and Rajasthan Under the chairmanship of Union Jal Shakti Minister Shri C. R. Patil, a solution to this problem was found in just a few days The framework that has been created for this agreement will remain established as a dispute-free agreement for many decades to come
In the presence of Union Home Minister and Minister of Cooperation Shri Amit Shah, the governments of Rajasthan and Haryana today signed an important agreement in New Delhi for the construction and implementation of the Yamuna Water Project. On this occasion, Rajasthan Chief Minister Shri Bhajan Lal Sharma, Haryana Chief Minister Shri Nayab Singh Saini, Union Jal Shakti Minister Shri C. R. Patil, and senior officials from the Central Government and the State Governments were present.
On this occasion, Union Home Minister and Minister of Cooperation Shri Amit Shah said in his address that this agreement has today resolved the nearly three-decade-old water-related problem faced by the people of Haryana and Rajasthan. He stated that this agreement is an excellent example of the mantra ‘Solution through Dialogue’, given by Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi. This agreement demonstrates that if states move forward with the spirit of cooperative federalism, even a problem persisting for three decades can be resolved easily.
Shri Amit Shah said that under today’s agreement, approximately 580 Million Cubic Meters (MCM) of water will be supplied from the Yamuna Canal to Rajasthan through three underground pipelines from July to October. These three pipelines have a diameter of more than 3.6 metres and will provide drinking water to the people of both Rajasthan and Haryana. Shri Shah said that this agreement is an excellent example of a Win-Win Situation for both states. The agreement has carefully addressed financial responsibilities, cost-sharing, water allocation, water release protocols, and maintenance arrangements. RBI Panaji organises Special Town Hall to mark International MSME Day at Verna
articles.full_content:
Reserve Bank of India (RBI), Panaji, observed the International MSME Day by organising a Special Town Hall for Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) at Verna Industrial Estate, Goa, on June 26, 2026.
The programme, organized by the Financial Inclusion and Development Department under the RBI, was inaugurated by Shri Krishnakumar S, Deputy General Manager, RBI, Panaji. In his address, Shri Krishnakumar talked about the significant contribution of MSMEs towards economic growth, employment generation and innovation. He also spoke on the importance of financial literacy, digital payments, cyber security and timely access to formal credit in strengthening the MSME ecosystem.
The Special Town Hall witnessed the participation of senior officials and representatives from NABARD, MSME Development and Facilitation Office, SIDBI, Goa Chamber of Commerce and Industry, ASSOCHAM, Khadi and Village Industries Commission, Goa Khadi and Village Industries Board, Central Bank of India, Bank of India, HDFC Bank, Axis Bank, State Bank of India, ICICI Bank, the Economic Development Corporation, Goa, and DRDA, besides several entrepreneurs and MSME stakeholders.
The programme featured interactive discussions on access to formal credit, responsible borrowing, digital payment systems, cyber security, grievance redress mechanisms and banking services for MSMEs. The event provided a platform to the entrepreneurs for engaging with officials from the RBI and participating institutions to discuss the issues related to credit and financial services.

NSO Chandigarh Celebrates 20th Statistics Day, Highlights Potential of Administrative Data
articles.full_content:
In recognition of the notable contributions made by Prof. P.C Mahalanobis in the field of Economics, Planning and Development of Statistics in India, Regional Office, National Statistics Office, Chandigarh celebrated the “20th Statistics Day” on 29th June, 2026 at Conference Hall, Kendriya Sadan, Sector-9, Chandigarh to commemorate the birth anniversary of the Father of Statistics “Late Shri P.C. Mahalanobis”. The theme for 20th Statistics Day was “Unlocking the Potential of Administrative Data”.
The program was graced by the presence of Sh. Harbinder Singh, ADG (Retd.) ISS Officer, NSO, Dr. Narinder Kumar, HoD , Department of Statistics, Panjab University and Ms. Sheenam Jain, Assistant Director, PIB Chandigarh as guest speakers.
In his welcome address, Sh. Deepak Mehra, Deputy Director General, NSO(FOD), RO Chandigarh welcomed all the guest speakers and officers/officials and interns from Panjab University and briefed about the biography of Shri. P.C. Mahalanobis and his contributions in the field of statistics.
Sh. Harbinder Singh, ADG (Retd.) ISS Officer, NSO inaugurated the event and also gave details of contributions of Prof. P.C. Mahalanobis in Indian Statistical System. He stressed upon the need to strengthen the statistical system in country. For this purpose, he opined that the theme of 20th Statistics Day i.e. “Unlocking the Potential of Administrative Data” would go a long way in achieving the goals.
The objective of this day was to create public awareness especially among the younger generation for drawing inspiration from Late Professor P.C. Mahalanobis about the role of statistics in socio-economic planning and policy formulation.
The function was attended by around 80 Officers & Officials and interns from Panjab University engaged in the field of Statistics.
To make the event successful, quiz competition was also organized in which interns from Panjab University and Survey Enumerators actively participated. Certificates were awarded to top three winners of quiz competition.
The event ended by Vote of thanks to all the guest speakers, Regional Head, Officers and Officials of NSO and interns from Panjab University.

articles.title: Indian Statistical Institute Celebrates 133rd Birth Anniversary of Prof. P.C. Mahalanobis and Statistics Day 2026
articles.full_content:
VC, Ashoka University highlights Growing Importance of Data & Statistical Research in Scientific Advancement
Kolkata, June 29, 2026
The Indian Statistical Institute (ISI), Kolkata, today celebrated the 133rd Birth Anniversary of Prof. Prasanta Chandra Mahalanobis, along with Statistics Day and Workers' Day, at its headquarters in Kolkata. The programme brought together eminent academicians, scientists, faculty members, researchers, students and staff to commemorate the remarkable legacy of the founder of the Indian Statistical Institute and pioneer of statistical science in India. The theme for Statistics Day 2026, announced by the Union Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation (MoSPI), is "Unlocking the Potential of Administrative Data." The programme was graced by Vice Chancellor, Ashoka University, Prof. Somak Raychaudhury as the Chief Guest. Former Acting Director and Faculty Member of ISI, Prof. Dipankar Dasgupta attended as the Special Guest. The event was also attended by Director, ISI, Prof. Amartya Kumar Dutta, President, ISI, Prof. Sankar Kumar Pal, Dean of Studies, Prof. Biswabrata Pradhan, Convener of the Reception Committee, Dr. Paramita Das, President, ISI Workers' Organisation, Dr. Partha Pratim Mohanta and Chief Executive (Administration & Finance), ISI, Shri Ravinder Kumar. Addressing the gathering, Prof. Amartya Kumar Dutta, highlighted the Institute's growth in recent years and its continuing reputation as one of India's premier institutions for research and higher education. He noted that ISI continues to enjoy immense national and international recognition for its excellence in statistics, mathematics, computer science, economics and interdisciplinary research, while maintaining the high standards envisioned by its founder. Prof. Sankar Kumar Pal, paid rich tributes to Prof. P.C. Mahalanobis, recalling his visionary leadership in establishing the Indian Statistical Institute and laying the foundation for statistical science in India. He observed that Prof. Mahalanobis' pioneering work transformed statistics into a vital instrument for scientific research, economic planning and public policy, leaving an enduring legacy that continues to guide the Institute. Delivering the keynote address, Prof. Somak Raychaudhury highlighted the growing importance of data and statistical science in modern scientific research, particularly in astronomy. He explained how astronomical discoveries today rely extensively on the analysis of massive volumes of observational data and emphasized that robust statistical methodologies have become indispensable for extracting meaningful scientific insights. He also acknowledged the significant contributions of ISI in advancing statistical research and developing scientific talent that has strengthened research across diverse disciplines. Varta in Kohima Highlights 12 Years of Government: Focus on Urban Livelihoods and Entrepreneurship
articles.full_content:
A Varta on the theme "12 Years of Government – From Empowerment to Prosperity: Urban Livelihoods and Entrepreneurship" was organised by the Press Information Bureau (PIB), Kohima, today at Hotel Japfu, Kohima. The programme brought together media representatives for an interactive session on the Government's urban livelihood initiatives and their impact in Nagaland.
The media interaction was addressed by Shri Petevilie Khatsu, Additional Director and State Mission Director, DAY-NULM, Directorate of Urban Development, Government of Nagaland, and Smt. Abau Soukhrie, State Mission Manager, DAY-NULM, Directorate of Urban Development, Government of Nagaland.
Delivering the theme address, Shri Petevilie Khatsu highlighted the transformative impact of the PM Street Vendor's AtmaNirbhar Nidhi (PM SVANidhi) scheme in empowering street vendors through affordable, collateral-free credit and enhanced social security. He stated that the scheme, launched in 2020 to support street vendors affected by the COVID-19 pandemic, has entered a new phase following its restructuring in January 2026. The revamped scheme has been extended until March 2030 and now promotes greater convergence with other government departments to strengthen financial inclusion and welfare delivery.
Explaining the scheme's key features, he informed that eligible street vendors can avail progressive working capital loans of ₹15,000, ₹25,000 and ₹50,000 based on timely repayment. Beneficiaries are also entitled to a 7 per cent interest subsidy, digital transaction incentives, government-backed credit guarantees, and a UPI-linked RuPay Credit Card after successful repayment of the second loan.
Shri Petevilie Khatsu further stated that PM SVANidhi extends beyond credit support through the SVANidhi Se Samriddhi initiative, which links beneficiaries to eight flagship welfare schemes, including life and accident insurance, pension, financial inclusion, maternity benefits and the One Nation One Ration Card scheme, thereby ensuring comprehensive socio-economic support for street vendors and their families.
Sharing Nagaland's progress under the scheme, he informed that the State has approved 6,511 loans worth over ₹10.75 crore, achieving 50 per cent of its target of 13,000 loans by 2030. Of these, 6,394 loans have been sanctioned, while 3,861 beneficiaries, representing around 60 per cent, have successfully repaid their loans, reflecting encouraging repayment discipline and the growing success of the scheme in the State.
He urged eligible street vendors to avail the benefits of PM SVANidhi, describing it as a significant initiative towards financial inclusion, entrepreneurship and sustainable urban livelihoods while reaffirming the Government's commitment to empowering street vendors with dignity and opportunity.

The Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation (MoSPI) commemorated the 20 th Statistics Day on 29th June 2026 at Dr. Ambedkar International Centre, New Delhi. The Statistics Day is celebrated annually in honour of the birth anniversary of Professor Prasanta Chandra Mahalanobis who was the architect of India's modern statistical system and a pioneer in economic planning.
The theme for this year’s Statistics Day was, “Unlocking the Potential of Administrative Data” , which underscored the growing importance of leveraging data generated through administrative processes for evidence-based policymaking and governance. The inaugural function was chaired by Dr. P. K. Mishra, Principal Secretary to Hon’ble Prime Minister and the chief guest on this occasion. Rao Inderjit Singh, Hon’ble Union Minister of State (Independent Charge) of MoSPI, Dr. Saurabh Garg, Secretary, MoSPI and Dr. Saibal Chattopadhyay , Chairperson, National Statistical Commission were also present during the event.
Shri P.R. Meshram, DG (Data Governance), in his welcome address talked about the various steps being taken by the Ministry in harmonization of administrative data and unlocking the same.
Chief Guest of the event, Dr. P. K. Mishra, Principal Secretary to Hon’ble Prime Minister, in his address recalled the emphasis being given by the Hon’ble PM in data driven decision making. He remarked that the theme of the 20 th Statistical Day marks inflection points of statistical system. Dr. Mishra further stated that the vast volume of digital information generated through public delivery systems, regulatory bodies, and sectoral databases must transition from a departmental byproduct into a cohesive, secure, and interoperable national asset. articles.title: Cabinet approves additional investment commitment of Rs. 30,000 crore in National Investment and Infrastructure Fund (NIIF) to accelerate infrastructure investment and catalyse institutional capital into India
articles.full_content:
With this, Government of India’s total commitment to NIIF now stands at Rs. 60,000 crore
In a significant step to deepen India’s investment commitment for infrastructure and other nationally important sectors, the Union Cabinet, under the leadership of Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi, has last week approved an additional Government of India’s investment commitment of Rs. 30,000 crore towards new and upcoming funds of the National Investment and Infrastructure Fund. This takes the Government of India’s total commitment to NIIF to Rs. 60,000 crore.
National Investment and Infrastructure Fund (NIIF) is India’s Sovereign Anchored Fund, professionally run and managed by National Investment and Infrastructure Fund Limited (NIIFL). GOI is 49% shareholder in NIIF and it currently manages capital commitments of approximately Rs. 40,000 crore across its funds and investment strategies. NIIF has demonstrated a strong track record of capital deployment and realisations, having returned close to Rs. 12,000 crore to investors through large portfolio exits.
NIIF has raised capital from marquee institutional investors, including Sovereign Wealth Funds, Pension Funds, Multilateral Development, and leading Domestic Financial Institutions. These include Abu Dhabi Investment Authority, AustralianSuper, CPP Investments, Ontario Teachers’ Pension Plan, PSP Investments, Temasek, Asian Infrastructure Investment Bank, New Development Bank, Asian Development Bank, Japan Bank for International Cooperation, U.S. International Development Finance Corporation, Axis Bank, HDFC Group, ICICI Bank, Kotak Mahindra Life Insurance and State Bank of India.
These investors span diverse geographies, including Australia, Canada, Japan, Singapore, the United Arab Emirates, and the United States, reflecting strong international confidence in India’s growth trajectory and NIIF’s governance and commercial track record
NIIF’s four operational investment strategies — infrastructure, private markets, growth equity, and climate investments in the India-Japan business corridor — have achieved notable investment momentum.
NIIF’s first infrastructure fund, with a corpus of Rs. 16000 crore, is India’s largest domestic infrastructure fund and has created platforms across transportation (roads, ports & logistics, and airports), energy (renewables, smart meters and power transmission) and digital infrastructure. Its Private Markets Fund has invested in multiple daughter AIFs managed by home-grown managers, which in turn have invested in areas such as climate, affordable housing, affordable healthcare, and venture capital (VC)/technologies. NIIF’s Strategic Opportunities Fund has focused on growth sectors such as financial services, healthcare and manufacturing. NIIF’s India-Japan Fund is its first bilateral fund and focuses on climate & circular economy and energy transition, as well as investments that further the India-Japan business corridor.

Union Health Minister Shri Jagat Prakash Nadda Launches ‘SUMAN Roadmap 2030’ to Strengthen Maternal and Newborn Healthcare
articles.full_content:
Comprehensive strategic framework charts India's roadmap towards achieving SDG targets for maternal and newborn health by 2030
Union Minister of Health and Family Welfare, Shri Jagat Prakash Nadda, today launched the SUMAN Roadmap 2030, a comprehensive and forward-looking strategic framework aimed at transforming maternal and newborn healthcare across the country and accelerating India's progress towards achieving the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) by 2030.
The Roadmap was unveiled during the 16th Conference of the Central Council of Health and Family Welfare (CCHFW) in the presence of Union Ministers of State for Health and Family Welfare, Smt. Anupriya Patel and Shri Prataprao Jadhav, Health Ministers from States and Union Territories, senior officials of the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, and other distinguished delegates.
India has registered significant gains in maternal and newborn health over the past decade through sustained policy interventions and strengthened healthcare delivery systems. However, despite this remarkable progress, maternal and newborn mortality continues to remain a challenge in certain geographies, particularly in high-focus States. Recognising the need for a more targeted and differentiated approach, the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare has developed the SUMAN Roadmap 2030, an evidence-driven strategy that combines national priorities with local realities to deliver equitable, high-quality maternal and newborn healthcare across the country.
Anchored in the RMNCHA+N framework, the Roadmap adopts a comprehensive life-cycle approach, integrating interventions from pre-pregnancy through pregnancy, childbirth and the postnatal period, while ensuring convergence with child health, adolescent health, family planning and nutrition programmes. The framework is designed to deliver seamless, person-centric care across every stage of the reproductive and maternal health continuum.
A defining feature of the Roadmap is its structured approach to the identification, tracking and management of high-risk pregnancies through four critical stages—antenatal care, third-trimester care, intrapartum care and the postnatal period—thereby enabling timely interventions and improved clinical outcomes. Drawing upon field experiences and evidence from programme implementation, the strategy also addresses challenges related to transportation, healthcare access in tribal and hard-to-reach areas, quality emergency obstetric care, community participation through SUMAN Panchayats, and the growing impact of climate change on maternal and newborn health.
To accelerate improvements where the burden remains highest, the Roadmap introduces focused, time-bound interventions across 130 districts in 13 high-focus States—Assam, Bihar, Chhattisgarh, Haryana, Jharkhand, Karnataka, Madhya Pradesh, Odisha, Punjab, Rajasthan, Uttar Pradesh, Uttarakhand and West Bengal—while simultaneously outlining strategies for all States and Union Territories

The Roadmap was unveiled during the 16th Conference of the Central Council of Health and Family Welfare (CCHFW) in the presence of Union Ministers of State for Health and Family Welfare, Smt. Anupriya Patel and Shri Prataprao Jadhav, Health Ministers from States and Union Territories, senior officials of the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, and other distinguished delegates.

Unified national programme to provide seamless continuum of home and community-based care from birth to 36 months
In a significant step towards strengthening newborn and child healthcare services across the country, Union Minister of Health and Family Welfare, Shri Jagat Prakash Nadda, today launched the Samagra Shishu Bal Swasthya Karyakram (SSBSK), a unified national programme that provides a seamless continuum of home and community-based care for every child from birth to 36 months of age.
The programme embodies the vision of "पहले तीन साल सम्पूर्ण देखभाल" (Comprehensive Care During the First Three Years) by integrating the existing Home-Based Newborn Care (HBNC) and Home-Based Care for Young Child (HBYC) programmes into a single comprehensive framework. The initiative recognises the critical importance of the first 28 days for newborn survival and the first three years for optimal brain development, while strengthening child survival, nutrition, healthy growth and early childhood development through an integrated approach.
A key feature of SSBSK is the introduction of a risk-stratified approach for newborns and young children identified as 'At-risk' due to conditions such as low birth weight, prematurity, delayed initiation of breastfeeding, discharge from newborn care units, malnutrition, recurrent illness or developmental delays. These children will receive intensified follow-up through additional home visits tailored to their level of risk. Under the programme, 'At-risk' newborns will receive up to nine home visits during the first 42 days of life, while 'At-risk' children will receive up to eight home visits up to the age of 36 months.
The programme further strengthens continuity of care through coordinated home visits by Accredited Social Health Activists (ASHAs), Auxiliary Nurse Midwives (ANMs), Community Health Officers (CHOs) and Anganwadi Workers (AWWs). Joint home visits by ANMs and CHOs have been incorporated on Day 3 and Day 7 for 'At-risk' newborns, and during the third and sixth months for 'At-risk' children. These coordinated interventions are designed to facilitate timely assessment, counselling and referral whenever required.
To strengthen early identification and management of vulnerable children, SSBSK introduces Well-Baby Sessions at every Village Health, Sanitation and Nutrition Day (VHSND) and a monthly Shishu Shivir at Ayushman Arogya Mandirs. These platforms will facilitate regular screening, developmental assessment and comprehensive management of newborns and young children within the community.
Recognising the close interdependence between maternal and child well-being, SSBSK incorporates post-partum maternal mental health screening as an integral component of community-based care. ASHAs will undertake early screening and facilitate timely referral for further assessment and support whenever necessary. NSO Nagpur Marks 20th Statistics Day, Highlights Role of Administrative Data in Governance
articles.full_content:
Nagpur, 29 June 2026
The National Statistical Office (NSO), under the Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation, Government of India, celebrated the 20th Statistics Day in Nagpur on Monday, highlighting the growing importance of administrative data in strengthening evidence-based governance and informed policymaking. The programme was jointly organised by the NSO's Zonal Office (Coordination & Quality Control Division), Regional Office (Field Operations Division) and Household Survey Unit at the Indian Bureau of Mines, Civil Lines.
The event commenced with floral tributes to the portrait of eminent statistician Prof. P.C. Mahalanobis, whose birth anniversary is observed as Statistics Day, followed by the ceremonial lighting of the lamp and screening of a documentary on his life and contributions to India's statistical system.
Delivering the welcome address, Shri Srinivas Uppala, Deputy Director General, NSO, described statistics as the "eyes and ears of the Government" and underscored the need to integrate administrative and survey data for effective governance and improved public service delivery. Referring to this year's theme, "Unlocking the Potential of Administrative Data for Decision-Making," he said robust data systems are essential for responsive and citizen-centric policymaking.
Shri Niyati Joshi, Deputy Director General, NSO, highlighted the increasing relevance of statistics in the era of Artificial Intelligence, Data Science and Big Data, and encouraged young statisticians to leverage emerging technologies while upholding the principles of accuracy and reliability in official statistics.
Guest speakers Dr. Aparna Samudra, Assistant Professor, Rashtrasant Tukadoji Maharaj Nagpur University, and Shri T.K. Basu, former Additional Director General, emphasized the ethical use of data and the transformative role of administrative data in research, governance and public welfare.
Poster-making, slogan writing, speech, quiz, crossword and word-search competitions were also organised as part of the celebration to encourage greater awareness among students and the public about the importance of statistics in national development.
PIB Nagpur | Shubham Gudadhe/Darshana Rane

NSO Organises Regional Training Camp for AIDIS, SAS and Migration Survey
articles.full_content:
81st Round Socio-Economic Survey scheduled from 1st July 2026 to 30th June 2027
The National Statistics Office (NSO), under the Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation, Government of India, will conduct the 81st Round of the Socio-Economic Survey on All India Debt and Investment Survey (AIDIS), Situation Assessment Survey (SAS) of Agricultural Households and Migration Particulars from 1st July, 2026 to 30th June, 2027.
The Regional Training Camp (RTC) was commenced on 22nd June 2026 at the National Institute of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (NiMSME), Yousufguda, Hyderabad and will continue up to 25th June 2026. Around 135 field staff members are participating in the training programme.
Smt. Praseeja P, Deputy Director General, Regional Office, Hyderabad inaugurated the RTC. In her address, she highlighted that public cooperation is a cornerstone of NSS surveys. By participating actively, citizens help ensure the collection of accurate and reliable data, which supports informed policymaking and contributes to national development.
IMPORTANCE OF THE UPCOMING SURVEYS:
Survey on Migration particulars The survey aims to generate reliable estimates on migration rates, reasons for migration, short-term migration, and other related characteristics of households and individuals across the country. The Survey on Migration is expected to provide comprehensive and up-to-date data on the extent and patterns of migration—both rural–urban and inter-state—along with reasons for migration, duration of stay, return migration, employment and income profiles of migrants, and the impact of migration on households left behind. The results will be instrumental in understanding labour mobility, urbanisation trends, remittance flows, and the social and economic integration of migrant populations. Such information will greatly benefit policymakers, planners, researchers, and development practitioners by enabling more targeted interventions in areas such as urban planning, housing, transportation, employment generation, social security, and skill development. The data will also help in assessing the implications of migration for inclusive growth and regional development, thereby strengthening India’s socio-economic policy framework. All India Debt and Investment Survey
All India Debt and Investment Survey (AIDIS) is one of the India’s most significant surveys on household finance. Its origins go back to the All India Rural Credit Survey (1951-52), later expanded to cover both debt and investment in 1961-62. Since then, the NSO has conducted AIDIS roughly once every decade, most recently in the 77th Round (2019) at the request of the Reserve Bank of India (RBI). The survey provides critical data on household indebtedness and asset ownership across both rural and urban areas. Its findings are instrumental in shaping national accounts, assessing inequality in asset distribution, understanding credit markets, and informing policies of the RBI, MoSPI, and othe

articles.full_content:
The chatbot enables beneficiaries to access essential PM-JAY services anytime and anywhere without the need to visit government offices or call centres
Union Minister of Health and Family Welfare, Shri Jagat Prakash Nadda launched the Ayushman Sarathi – PM-JAY Whatsapp Chatbot, here today. Developed by the National Health Authority (NHA), under the Ministry of Health & Family Welfare, “Ayushman Sarathi” will provide citizens with easy, secure, and 24×7 access to Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB PM-JAY) services through WhatsApp.
The chatbot enables beneficiaries to access essential PM-JAY services anytime and anywhere without the need to visit government offices or call centres. Built on secure API-based integrations with PM-JAY systems, Ayushman Sarathi delivers real-time information and services directly on WhatsApp.
Key Services Available
Ayushman Sarathi offers a wide range of beneficiary-centric services, including:
• Eligibility Check under PM-JAY
• My Ayushman Card Apply for Ayushman Card Download Ayushman Card Re-do eKYC Link Aadhaar Lock / Unlock PM-JAY Card
• Apply for Ayushman Card
• Download Ayushman Card
• Lock / Unlock PM-JAY Card
• Ayushman Vaya Vandana Card (70+ Years)
• View Wallet Balance
• View Treatment History
• Find Nearby Empanelled Hospitals
• Register, Track and Withdraw Grievances
• Request a Call back
• Feedback on Discharge
Enhancing Beneficiary Experience
Ayushman Sarathi has been designed to improve accessibility, transparency and citizen engagement by:
• Providing instant access to PM-JAY services through WhatsApp.
• Enabling real-time API-based service delivery.
• Reducing dependency on physical visits and call centres.
• Supporting timely grievance registration and beneficiary assistance.
• Facilitating digital feedback collection after hospital discharge to strengthen service quality monitoring.
Digital Governance for Better Healthcare
The chatbot serves as an important digital interface between PM-JAY beneficiaries and the National Health Authority, promoting efficient service delivery and improved beneficiary satisfaction. The platform also supports data-driven governance by enabling feedback collection, grievance management, and continuous improvement in healthcare service delivery.
Ayushman Sarathi represents another significant step towards making healthcare services under Ayushman Bharat more accessible, transparent and citizen-centric through the use of digital technologies. How to Access Ayushman Sarathi?
Beneficiaries can access the official PM-JAY WhatsApp Chatbot – Ayushman Sarathi by scanning the QR code below or by sending “Hi” on WhatsApp to +91 72908 23838 . The chatbot is available 24×7 and provides secure, API-based access to PM-JAY services.

articles.title: Union Health Minister Shri Jagat Prakash Nadda Launches Drug Registry to standardize medicine-related information across the healthcare ecosystem
articles.full_content:
Drug Registry will enable transparency, trust and seamless access to drug information across the nation: A stronger digital backbone for India’s healthcare future
Medicines are one of the most critical data elements in healthcare delivery. However, across healthcare systems in India, the same drug is often represented using different names and formats, leading to inconsistencies, duplication, data entry errors, and lack of interoperability. This creates challenges in clinical decision-making, e-prescriptions, supply chain management, and continuity of care.
To address this gap, Union Minister of Health and Family Welfare, Shri Jagat Prakash Nadda launched the Drug Registry as a unified and standardized digital platform for drug-related information. Conceptualized under the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM), it serves as a single source of truth for medicines in India , enabling consistent identification, storage, exchange and usage of drug data across healthcare systems.
ABDM is building a robust digital health ecosystem through four core registries:
• ABHA Registry (for individuals)
• Healthcare Professional Registry (HPR)
• Health Facility Registry (HFR)
• Drug Registry (for medicines)
The Drug Registry has been developed in collaboration with the Central Drugs Standard Control Organization (CDSCO) and National Resource Centre for EHR Standards (NRCeS), Pune. It leverages international standards such as SNOMED CT to ensure interoperability and semantic consistency across platforms.
The registry includes standardized drug codes covering:
• Generic (clinical drugs)
• Branded medicines
Currently, the Drug Registry comprises:
• 123,000+ branded drugs
• 10,000+ generic drugs
• 29,000+ substances
The platform enables users to search and identify drugs using:
Built on standardized terminology and aligned with global standards, the Drug Registry ensures accuracy, transparency, and seamless data exchange across healthcare systems.
The Drug Registry is designed to integrate with:
• Hospital Management Information Systems (HMIS)
• E-prescription platforms
• Doctor-facing applications
• ABDM-compliant digital health solutions
It provides open APIs for seamless integration, thereby strengthening interoperability across India’s digital healthcare ecosystem. The registry connects healthcare providers, digital applications, and citizens to a verified and comprehensive drug database.
Drug Registry Portal Link: https://drugregistry.abdm.gov.in
Drug Registry Sandbox/Testing Portal Link: https://drugregistrysbx.abdm.gov.in/dr/v3

BRICS Chamber of Commerce & Industry (BRICS CCI) calls Dr. Jitendra Singh
articles.full_content:
Discusses with Minister the ways to strengthen industry-led global engagement and collaboration across the BRICS and BRICS Plus ecosystem India's Space and Biotechnology Sectors Present New Collaboration Opportunities: Dr. Jitendra Singh
A delegation of the BRICS Chamber of Commerce & Industry (BRICS CCI) called on Union Minister of State (Independent Charge) for Science & Technology, Earth Sciences; Minister of State for the Prime Minister's Office, Personnel, Public Grievances & Pensions; Department of Atomic Energy and Department of Space, Dr. Jitendra Singh today and discussed ways to strengthen industry-led global engagement and collaboration across the BRICS and BRICS Plus ecosystem through innovation, StartUps, emerging technologies, research collaboration and entrepreneurship.
The delegation, led by Mr. Sameep Shastri, Chairman of BRICS CCI, included Mr. Atul Bansal, Vice Chairman; Ms. Shabana Nasim, Vice Chairperson; and other office-bearers of the Chamber. The representatives briefed the Minister on the Chamber's initiatives over the past decade to promote trade facilitation, business-to-business engagement, women entrepreneurship, startup networking, Artificial Intelligence, research partnerships and sectoral cooperation among BRICS and BRICS Plus countries.
Dr. Jitendra Singh said that India's growing stature as a global innovation hub and its leadership in emerging technologies provide an ideal opportunity for industry organisations to build stronger international partnerships that contribute to national growth and global development. He said institutions working in the BRICS space should strive to create a distinct value proposition through knowledge, innovation and meaningful partnerships rather than limiting themselves to conventional networking platforms.
Referring to India's expanding innovation ecosystem, Dr. Jitendra Singh said the country has witnessed remarkable progress in sectors such as Space, Biotechnology, Artificial Intelligence, Nuclear Energy and Deep Technology, opening new avenues for global collaboration. He said reforms introduced over the last decade have transformed these sectors into engines of economic growth and entrepreneurship, creating significant opportunities for partnerships involving government, industry, academia and innovators.
The Minister said industry platforms such as BRICS CCI can facilitate collaborations between Indian StartUps and innovation ecosystems across BRICS and BRICS Plus countries while promoting technology-led economic engagement.
Dr. Jitendra Singh encouraged the Chamber to deepen its engagement with the wider BRICS ecosystem through sector-specific meetings, innovation forums and thematic engagements. He said sustained visibility, meaningful participation and knowledge-based contributions would further strengthen the Chamber's role in advancing India's engagement with the expanding BRICS ecosystem.

articles.title: India's Index of industrial production records growth of 5.1% in May 2026
articles.full_content:
QUICK ESTIMATES OF ALL INDIA INDEX OF INDUSTRIAL PRODUCTION (IIP) BASE YEAR 2022-23 FOR MAY 2026
• MoSPI has decided to adopt Output PPI as Deflator in place of WPI for item groups for which output is collected in value terms.
• This affects 234 out of the 463 item groups in the IIP basket, representing 36.02 per cent of the total index weight.
• MoSPI has now revised and released the entire IIP 2022-23 series with Output PPI and it supersedes the earlier WPI based IIP 2022–23 series released on 1 st June 2026.
In May 2026, Index of Industrial Production recorded a 5.1% year-on-year growth, supported by 5.5% growth in Manufacturing sector and strong growth of 9.9% in Electricity & Gas Supply sector.
The Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation (MoSPI) released the new series of the All India Index of Industrial Production (IIP) with base year 2022–23 on 1 st June 2026, using the Wholesale Price Index (WPI) as the deflator. Subsequently, the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT), Ministry of Commerce and Industry, released the Output Producer Price Index (Output PPI) series with base year 2022–23 on 15 th June 2026. As output PPI is a new and important indicator for capturing producer level prices, it has significant implications for compiling IIP.
Reasons for adoption of Output PPI
MoSPI has examined the adoption of output PPI in place of WPI in light of the following factors:
• The transition from WPI to Output PPI assumes significance because a part of industrial production in the IIP is reported in value terms. Out of the 463 item groups included in the IIP basket, 234 item groups, accounting for 36.02 per cent of the total weight, are compiled using value-based production data.
The transition from WPI to Output PPI assumes significance because a part of industrial production in the IIP is reported in value terms. Out of the 463 item groups included in the IIP basket, 234 item groups, accounting for 36.02 per cent of the total weight, are compiled using value-based production data.
• Output PPI provides a more granular price structure than the WPI. For value based items, use of Output PPI will improve the estimation of real output.

DGTS Commemorates Successful Completion of 9 Years of GST; Reaffirms Commitment to Taxpayer Facilitation and Trade
articles.full_content:
The Directorate General of Taxpayer Services (DGTS), Ahmedabad Zonal Unit, will celebrate the successful completion of nine years of the Goods and Services Tax (GST) by organizing a seminar/webinar on "GST Awareness & Overview" on 30 June 2026 at JG University, Asia Campus, Drive-in Road, Near Himalaya Mall, Thaltej, Ahmedabad . The seminar/webinar is being organised in association with JG University as part of DGTS's continuing efforts to promote taxpayer awareness, voluntary compliance, and stakeholder engagement.
The seminar/webinar will offer participants with an overview of the GST framework, recent developments, compliance requirements, and various taxpayer facilitation initiatives undertaken by the Government. The programme will also highlight the transformative impact of GST over the past nine years in simplifying indirect taxation, promoting transparency, and enhancing the ease of doing business.
The seminar/webinar will be addressed by Shri Sumit Kumar, IRS, Principal Additional Director General, Directorate General of Taxpayer Services (DGTS), Ahmedabad Zonal Unit, who will share insights on the evolution of GST over the last nine years and the role of DGTS in promoting taxpayer awareness, grievance redressal, and stakeholder facilitation. The seminar/webinar will provide how GST has emerged as one of the most significant economic reforms in the country by integrating multiple indirect taxes into a unified tax structure, creating a common national market, improving transparency, simplifying compliance, and strengthening the ease of doing business.
The seminar/webinar aims to emphasize the pivotal role played by taxpayers, trade, industry, tax professionals, and other stakeholders in the successful implementation and continuous evolution of the GST regime. It will also highlight how the collaborative partnership between the Government and stakeholders has significantly strengthened voluntary compliance, enhanced the efficiency of tax administration, and fostered a culture of transparency, trust, and shared responsibility.
The programme will also highlight the role of the Directorate General of Taxpayer Services (DGTS) in strengthening taxpayer facilitation through awareness programmes, grievance redressal, stakeholder consultations, and continuous outreach initiatives. Participants will be apprised of the various taxpayer support mechanisms available through DGTS and encouraged to actively engage with the Directorate for addressing grievances, providing feedback, and contributing to the continual improvement of taxpayer services.
Students, taxpayers, entrepreneurs, trade and industry representatives, tax professionals, academicians, and other stakeholders are invited to participate in the programme. ICAR-Sikkim Centre Engages with Farmers at Kussong Under MGMG & KBA to Promote Climate-Resilient Hill Farming
articles.full_content:
Mangan, 29th June 2026
Under the Mera Gaon Mera Gaurav (MGMG) programme and Khet Bachao Abhiyan (KBA), the ICAR Research Complex for North Eastern Hill Region, Sikkim Centre, conducted a Farmer–Scientist Interaction Programme at Kussong village in Mangan District on Sunday, 28th June 2026. Kussong is adopted under the Tribal Sub Plan.
Seventeen farmers from the village took part in the programme, which aimed to promote sustainable agricultural practices, efficient resource use, and climate-resilient farming systems tailored for hill ecosystems.
The program was facilitated under the guidance of Dr. Ashok Kumar, Head of Regional Centre (HoRC), along with Dr. Saurav Saha, Senior Scientist (Agricultural Physics).
Speaking to farmers, Dr. Ashok Kumar stressed the need to adopt improved agricultural technologies, integrated farming systems, and resource-conserving methods to improve productivity and secure rural livelihoods. He called upon farmers to leverage scientific interventions for achieving sustainable agricultural growth.
In the technical segment, Dr. Saurav Saha detailed the role of mulching in conserving soil moisture, managing weeds, and enhancing soil health. He elaborated on the effective use of organic resources to sustain crop yields. Guidance was also given on scientific vegetable cultivation, proper nursery management, and selection of quality planting materials to ensure higher productivity and better produce quality. He underlined the principles of Integrated Nutrient Management (INM), advocating balanced application of organic manures, compost, biofertilizers, and green manure to maintain soil fertility and support crop performance.
The team carried out a field visit to examine crop conditions and hold direct interactions with farmers. Following on-the-spot assessments, the scientists shared practical, location-specific advice on crop management, nutrient application, moisture conservation, and resource-efficient farming.
An interactive discussion marked the close of the programme, where farmers presented field-level issues and received expert solutions. The scientists confirmed that technical handholding and support will be continued under the MGMG programme and Khet Bachao Abhiyan.

articles.title: Madhya Pradesh becomes the nation's model for the cheapest green energy; Union Minister Pralhad Joshi.
articles.full_content:
Union Minister Pralhad Joshi and Chief Minister Dr. Mohan Yadav inaugurate 950 MW solar parks. Neemuch (500 MW) and Shajapur (450 MW) solar parks dedicated to the nation; electricity will be available at a record minimum rate of ₹2.14 per unit. "Madhya Pradesh is accelerating Prime Minister Modi’s Net-Zero vision and the country's energy self-reliance." — Union Minister Pralhad Joshi.
Union Minister for New and Renewable Energy, Shri Pralhad Joshi, and Madhya Pradesh Chief Minister, Dr. Mohan Yadav, elevated Madhya Pradesh's solar energy sector to a new and historic height during a grand event today. Both leaders jointly dedicated two massive solar power plants with a total capacity of 950 MW to the nation—the 500 MW capacity Neemuch Solar Park and the 450 MW capacity Shajapur Solar Park.
The State Minister for New and Renewable Energy, Shri Rakesh Shukla, was also present on this occasion. Following the inauguration, Union Minister Shri Pralhad Joshi congratulated the Government of Madhya Pradesh, electricity consumers, and the citizens of the state, stating that Madhya Pradesh has set a new benchmark for the entire nation in terms of solar energy tariffs.
The Union Minister remarked, "Today is a milestone in the history of India's Green Energy Transition. Tariffs of ₹2.14 to ₹2.15 per unit for the Neemuch Solar Park and ₹2.33 to ₹2.35 per unit for the Shajapur Solar Park have been achieved, which are among the lowest and most highly competitive rates across the entire country."
Appreciating the agreement signed for the state's first solar storage project (the Morena Project), the Union Minister stated that merely generating solar power is not enough; battery storage technology is crucial to keep the grid stable during the night or times of need. He added that achieving the country's lowest rate of ₹2.70 per unit (including storage) for the Morena Project proves that India is now leading the world in both technology and financial viability.
Alongside the inauguration of the solar projects, Chief Minister Dr. Mohan Yadav and Union Minister Shri Pralhad Joshi also performed the 'Bhoomi Pujan' (groundbreaking ceremony) and laid the foundation stones for various industrial units and development projects worth ₹1,593.98 crore to boost industrial development and employment in the state. The Union Minister reiterated that Madhya Pradesh has emerged as a leading state in fulfilling Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi's 'Panchamrit' goals and the resolve to make India 'Net-Zero' (zero carbon emissions) by the year 2070.
articles.summary: Union Minister Pralhad Joshi and Chief Minister Dr. Neemuch (500 MW) and Shajapur (450 MW) solar parks dedicated to the nation; electricity will be available at a record minimum rate of ₹2.14 per unit. "Madhya Pradesh is accelerating Prime Minister Modi’s Net-Zero vision and the country's energy self-reliance." — Union Minister Pralhad Joshi.

articles.title: WWF-EIACP Highlights Advanced Geospatial Analysis for Forest Fire Management at IIFM, Bhopal
articles.full_content:
The WWF-Environmental Information, Awareness, Capacity Building and Livelihood Programme Centre (WWF-EIACP PC-RP), functioning under the aegis of the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEF&CC) and entrusted with the pan-India thematic area "Wildlife Conservation, including Species and Habitats using Geospatial Techniques," organised a Forest Fire Awareness and Capacity Building Programme at the Indian Institute of Forest Management (IIFM), Bhopal.
The programme was conducted under the guidance of Dr. K. Ravichandran, Director, IIFM, marking the first collaborative initiative between WWF-EIACP PC-RP and IIFM. The initiative brought together scientific research, geospatial technology and environmental awareness to strengthen forest fire preparedness and promote sustainable forest management.
An awareness session was delivered by Dr. G. Areendran, Coordinator, WWF-EIACP PC-RP, who highlighted the application of advanced geospatial analysis for strengthening forest fire prevention, monitoring and management in Madhya Pradesh. The session presented scientific assessments of forest fire trends, emerging hotspots, environmental and anthropogenic drivers, forest fire susceptibility mapping, and an interactive Forest Fire Dashboard designed to support evidence-based decision-making.
The analysis revealed that forest fire incidences in Madhya Pradesh are predominantly concentrated between February and April, highlighting the need for enhanced preparedness and preventive measures during the peak fire season.
Based on forest fire data analysed for the period 2012–2025 using the Mann-Kendall trend test, the overall trend in forest fire occurrences across the state remained statistically stable. However, several districts have emerged as new hotspots, including Neemuch, Gwalior, Shivpuri, Sheopur, Sagar, Guna and Morena, while intermittent hotspot activity was observed in Chhindwara, Balaghat, Raisen, Mandla and Betul. The study further identified East Nimar, Raisen, Balaghat, Chhindwara, Dewas and Singrauli as districts consistently recording high incidences of forest fires during the study period.
The findings highlighted that forest fire occurrence is strongly associated with mid-elevation landscapes (266–533 metres), gentle slopes, south-facing aspects, temperatures between 30°C and 38°C, low-to-moderate rainfall and humidity levels, and areas located in close proximity to roads and human settlements.
A burnt-area assessment identified 2022 as the most severe fire year during the study period, with an estimated 1,809.69 square kilometres of forest area affected by fire.
The study has generated practical decision-support tools, including forest fire susceptibility maps, hotspot classification and an interactive Forest Fire Dashboard, which can assist Forest Departments in prioritising high-risk areas and strengthening preparedness, prevention and response strategies.

Union Ministry of Health and Family Welfare Proposes Amendments to Medical Devices Rules, 2017 to Streamline Licensing Process
articles.full_content:
Draft amendments aim to reduce timelines for grant of manufacturing licences and enhance ease of doing business
The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare has published a draft notification in the Official Gazette proposing amendments to the Medical Devices Rules, 2017 with the objective of simplifying and expediting the licensing process for medical devices while ensuring continued compliance with quality, safety and performance requirements.
The proposed amendments seek to rationalize the timelines for grant of manufacturing licences for medical devices across different risk categories. The initiative is aimed at enhancing the ease of doing business, improving regulatory efficiency, and facilitating the timely availability of quality medical devices in the country.
Under the Medical Devices Rules, 2017, medical devices are classified into four risk-based categories—Class A, Class B, Class C and Class D—with Class D comprising the highest-risk devices. The Rules prescribe statutory timelines for processing applications for manufacturing licences for each category. The proposed amendments seek to reduce these timelines, thereby enabling faster regulatory approvals while maintaining the established standards of quality, safety and performance.
For Class B medical devices, which include low to moderate risk devices such as blood pressure monitors, hypodermic needles and pulse oximeters, the timeline for grant of manufacturing licence has been proposed to be reduced from 140 days to 115 days.
Similarly, for Class C and Class D medical devices, which include high-risk devices such as cardiac stents, hip and knee implants, and other orthopaedic implants, the timeline for grant of manufacturing licence has been proposed to be reduced from 105 days to 90 days.
The draft amendments also introduce clearly defined timelines for each stage of the licensing process, including scrutiny of applications, audit by notified bodies, verification of compliance and issuance of licences. This is expected to bring greater transparency, predictability and efficiency to the regulatory framework, benefiting both the medical device industry and patients through faster access to quality-assured medical devices.
The draft notification has been placed in the public domain for comments and suggestions from all stakeholders. The notification is available in the Official Gazette and on the website of the Central Drugs Standard Control Organisation (CDSCO). Stakeholders are invited to submit their comments and suggestions within the prescribed period.
The draft Gazette Notification is available at: https://egazette.gov.in/WriteReadData/2026/273896.pdf
HFW / Amendments to Medical Devices Rules, 2017 /28 June 2026/1

The proposed amendments seek to rationalize the timelines for grant of manufacturing licences for medical devices across different risk categories. The initiative is aimed at enhancing the ease of doing business, improving regulatory efficiency, and facilitating the timely availability of quality medical devices in the country.

DCIL Joins the Prime Minister's Employment Mission with Successful Three-Day Recruitment Drive
articles.full_content:
Reinforcing its commitment to nation-building through employment generation, Dredging Corporation of India Limited (DCIL) successfully organized a three-day Rozgar Mela from June 23 to June 25, 2026, at its Head Office in Visakhapatnam. Organized by the Human Resources Department, the recruitment drive witnessed an overwhelming response, with more than 500 aspiring candidates from across the country participating in the selection process for various categories of fleet personnel.
The initiative was conducted in alignment with the Government of India's Rozgar Mela mission, reflecting the vision of the Hon'ble Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi to create meaningful employment opportunities across sectors. The programme also received the encouragement and support of the Hon'ble Union Minister of Ports, Shipping and Waterways, Shri Sarbananda Sonowal, and the Hon'ble Minister of State for Ports, Shipping and Waterways, Shri Shantanu Thakur. As India's premier dredging organization, jointly owned by a consortium of four major Port Authorities under the Ministry of Ports, Shipping and Waterways (MoPSW), DCIL continues to play a vital role in strengthening the country's maritime infrastructure while creating quality employment opportunities for skilled professionals.
The Rozgar Mela was organized under the guidance of Shri Jasmeet Singh Bindra, IRTS, Chairman, DCIL, and Capt. S. Divakar, Managing Director & CEO, DCIL, whose leadership continues to drive the organization towards operational excellence and human resource development.
Shri Jasmeet Singh Bindra, IRTS, Chairman, DCIL, said: "The overwhelming response to this recruitment drive reflects the growing confidence of India's youth in the maritime sector and in DCIL. As India's premier dredging organisation under the Ministry of Ports, Shipping and Waterways, we remain committed to supporting the Hon'ble Prime Minister's vision of employment generation by creating meaningful career opportunities and nurturing skilled maritime professionals for the nation."
Capt. S. Divakar, Managing Director & CEO, DCIL, said: "The success of this Rozgar Mela demonstrates the availability of talented professionals ready to contribute to India's maritime growth. DCIL will continue to organise similar recruitment drives throughout the year to build a competent, future-ready workforce capable of meeting the evolving needs of the maritime sector while delivering operational excellence."
The successful completion of the Rozgar Mela once again demonstrates DCIL's commitment not only to developing world-class maritime infrastructure but also to empowering India's youth through sustainable employment opportunities. The initiative marks another significant milestone in DCIL's journey towards supporting the vision of 'Viksit Bharat' through a stronger, skilled and future-ready maritime workforce.

Union Minister of Commerce and Industry Shri Piyush Goyal Leads High-Level Business Delegation to Greece Following Successful UK Engagements
articles.full_content:
India–Greece Startup Interactions to Promote Cross-Border Innovation Partnerships High-Level Business Dialogue to Advance India–Greece Investment and Industrial Cooperation
Union Minister of Commerce and Industry Shri Piyush Goyal is now leading a high-level Indian business delegation to Athens, Greece, following his successful engagements in the United Kingdom, with a focus on expanding bilateral trade, investment flows and strategic economic collaboration.
Shri Goyal concluded his visit to the United Kingdom with the India–UK: Partners in Progress Business Plenary held in London. The engagement reaffirmed the strong momentum in bilateral economic relations, with leading industry stakeholders from both countries expressing firm support for the proposed Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA) as a transformative framework for deepening trade and investment ties.
The engagements in the United Kingdom, followed by the ongoing visit to Greece, reflect the Government of India's continued commitment under the leadership of Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi to strengthening global economic partnerships, fostering innovation-led growth, and advancing India's trade and investment diplomacy in key international markets.
During the visit to Greece, the delegation will participate in presentations and startup pitches at The Athens Startup Business Incubator (THEA), providing a platform to showcase next-generation enterprises and promote cross-border innovation partnerships.
The delegation will also hold focused roundtable discussions with Indian and Greek industry leaders at the Athens Chamber of Commerce and Industry (ACCI). The discussions will cover key sectors, including infrastructure, digitalisation and defence, and food and agriculture, with a view to identifying new avenues for collaboration and strengthening private-sector partnerships.
The programme will include a high-level business interaction bringing together leading stakeholders from both countries to explore opportunities for enhanced investment and commercial partnerships across priority sectors. The delegation will also visit leading Greek enterprises to explore collaboration in advanced manufacturing and technology-driven sectors, strengthening industrial synergies between the two countries.
The visit will conclude with interactions involving key business and institutional stakeholders aimed at deepening industry partnerships and expanding bilateral trade linkages.
